title: Gulbarga Fort
tagline: Gulbarga Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: The Gulbarga Fort is located in Kalaburagi in the Kalaburagi district of North Karnataka.
overview.description: The Gulbarga Fort is located in Kalaburagi in the Kalaburagi district of North Karnataka. The fort was originally constructed by the Hindu monarch Raja Gulchand, and it was subsequently significantly enlarged in 1347 by Ala-ud-Din Bahman Shah of the Bahmani Kingdom after he cut off his ties with the Delhi Sultanate. Islamic monuments such as mosques, palaces, tombs, and other structures were also built later within the refurbished fort. The Jama Masjid, built later within the fort in 1367, is a unique structure built in the Persian architectural style, fully enclosed with elegant domes and arc

topPlaces.name: Jama Mosque, Kalaburagi
topPlaces.description: The Jama Masjid, also known as the Friday Mosque of Gulbarga, the Great Mosque of Gulbarga Fort, and formally as the Jamia Masjid - Qila-e-Hasham, is a Friday mosque located in Kalaburagi (formerly known as Gulbarga), in the state of Karnataka, India. The mosque is located within the Gulbarga Fort complex, in Kalaburagi.

topPlaces.name: Kalaburagi
topPlaces.description: Kalaburagi, formerly known as Gulbarga, is a city in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is headquarters of the eponymous Kalaburagi district and Kalaburagi division.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Gulbarga
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Gulbarga in India was created on 24 June 2005. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Haft Gumbaz
topPlaces.description: The Haft Gumbaz, also spelt Haft Gumbad are a group of tombs of the Bahmani dynasty situated in Gulbarga, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Built during the 14th and 15th centuries, the tombs are examples of early Indo-Islamic architecture.

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Kalaburagi
topPlaces.description: Government Museum, Kalaburagi is a museum in Kalaburagi, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is run by the state government.

topPlaces.name: Chor Gumbad
topPlaces.description: Chor Gumbad, Gulbarga is a domed building in Gumbad, Kalaburagi. It is listed as a state protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Langar Ki Masjid
topPlaces.description: The Langar Ki Masjid, also known as Langar Masjid or Langar Mosque, is a former mosque, now in partial ruins, situated in Kalaburagi, in the state of Karnataka, India. The former mosque is a state protected monument.

items.note: The Jama Masjid, also known as the Friday Mosque of Gulbarga, the Great Mosque of Gulbarga Fort, and formally as the Jamia Masjid - Qila-e-Hasham, is a Friday mosque located in Kalaburagi (formerly known as Gulbarga), in the state of Karnataka, India. The mosque is located within the Gulbarga Fort complex, in Kalaburagi.
Kalaburagi, formerly known as Gulbarga, is a city in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is headquarters of the eponymous Kalaburagi district and Kalaburagi division.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Gulbarga in India was created on 24 June 2005. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Bangalore.

The Haft Gumbaz, also spelt Haft Gumbad are a group of tombs of the Bahmani dynasty situated in Gulbarga, in the Indian state of Karnataka. Built during the 14th and 15th centuries, the tombs are examples of early Indo-Islamic architecture.
Government Museum, Kalaburagi is a museum in Kalaburagi, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is run by the state government.
Chor Gumbad, Gulbarga is a domed building in Gumbad, Kalaburagi. It is listed as a state protected monument.

The Langar Ki Masjid, also known as Langar Masjid or Langar Mosque, is a former mosque, now in partial ruins, situated in Kalaburagi, in the state of Karnataka, India. The former mosque is a state protected monument.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gulbarga Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gulbarga Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gulbarga Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (170 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gulbarga Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gulbarga Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gulbarga Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Gulbarga Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gulbarga Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jama Mosque, Kalaburagi, Kalaburagi, Diocese of Gulbarga, Haft Gumbaz, Government Museum, Kalaburagi.
